discipline.core: No PGA in the intrinsic vulnerability score.
discipline.event: PGA is introduced only in event-damage scenario mode.
discipline.priority: ADT changes prioritization and consequence, not structural vulnerability.
discipline.ndvi: NDVI remains an optional contextual modifier rather than a structural core driver.

priors.label: Condition rating
priors.rationale: Condition remains the clearest bridge-health signal in inspection-led vulnerability screening.

priors.label: SVI
priors.rationale: The revised Seismic Vulnerability Index aggregates multiple structural vulnerability factors into one interpretable score.

priors.label: Bridge age / design era
priors.rationale: Older bridges are more likely to reflect earlier seismic detailing assumptions and legacy configurations.

priors.label: Reconstruction timing
priors.rationale: More recent reconstruction or rehabilitation generally reduces expected vulnerability relative to peers of similar age.

priors.label: Skew angle
priors.rationale: Skew is consistently treated as a meaningful irregularity driver in bridge seismic vulnerability and fragility studies.

priors.label: Maximum span length
priors.rationale: Longer spans tend to imply greater demand concentration and more consequential structural response when detailing is weak.

priors.label: Bridge class / HWB class
priors.rationale: Bridge class preserves important system-level fragility distinctions without directly importing hazard intensity into the intrinsic score.

priors.label: Number of spans
priors.rationale: More spans increase geometry complexity, support interaction, and the chance of uneven seismic demand distribution.

layers.label: Structural / age / geometry inputs
layers.description: Year built, rehab timing, spans, max span, skew, bridge class, condition, and SVI.

layers.label: Normalized vulnerability indicators
layers.description: Bridge-specific variables are normalized into interpretable intrinsic vulnerability components.

layers.label: Prototype vulnerability score
layers.description: A transparent research prior layer combines those indicators without using PGA.

layers.label: Consequence and prioritization
layers.description: ADT and optional NDVI adjustments appear later in inspection prioritization rather than baseline vulnerability.

references.title: Mangalathu et al. (2019) - Rapid seismic damage evaluation of bridges using machine learning techniques
references.note: Supports the use of bridge-specific structural features and ML for bridge seismic damage evaluation.

references.title: Gorishniy et al. (2021) - Revisiting deep learning models for tabular data
references.note: Motivates strong tabular baselines and careful comparison across model families instead of assuming deep learning is always best.

references.title: Shwartz-Ziv and Armon (2022) - Tabular data: Deep learning is not all you need
references.note: Supports the use of interpretable tree-based and boosted tabular models in engineering-data settings.
